zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabella Dakeney於大約1311年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾時，她的父親Roger Dakeney33歲，母親Joan Cave28歲。她於1337年與Gerard de Braybrooke在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1388年在她的家鄉享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabella Dakeney于大约1311年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾时，她的父亲Roger Dakeney33岁，母亲Joan Cave28岁。她于1337年与Gerard de Braybrooke在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1388年在她的家乡享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: Dakeney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Hackney
meanings: habitational name from Hackney in Greater London, named from an Old English personal name Haca (genitive Hacan) + ēg ‘island, dry ground in marshland’.

perhaps from Middle English hakenei (Old French haquenée), an ambling horse or mare, especially one considered suitable for women to ride; perhaps therefore a metonymic occupational name for a breeder or keeper of such horses and short for Hakeneyman. This surname has also been found in Scotland since medieval times.

nameMeanings.name: Isabella
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabella Dakeney was born about 1311, in Buckinghamshire, England, her father, Roger Dakeney, was 33 and her mother, Joan Cave, was 28. She married Gerard de Braybrooke in 1337, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died about 1388, in her hometown, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabella Dakeney est née environ 1311, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger Dakeney, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Joan Cave, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Gerard de Braybrooke en 1337, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée environ 1388, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabella Dakeney um 1311 in Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Dakeney 33 und ihre Mutter Joan Cave 28. Sie hat 1337 in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom Gerard de Braybrooke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1388 im Alter von 79 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabella Dakeney è nata il circa 1311 nel Buckinghamshire, England, sua padre, Roger Dakeney, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Joan Cave, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Gerard de Braybrooke nel 1337, nel Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1388, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabella Dakeneyが約1311年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父Roger Dakeneyは33歳，母Joan Caveは28歳であった。1337年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアでGerard de Braybrookeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1388年に故郷で79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabella Dakeney(이)가 대략 1311년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Dakeney(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Cave(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 1337년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 Gerard de Braybrooke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1388년 그녀의 고향에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabella Dakeney родилась около 1311 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger Dakeney было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Joan Cave, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Gerard de Braybrooke в 1337 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла около 1388 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 79 лет.
